remarkList.remark:
Sonangol Njinga Mbande was delivered to Mercuria straight from N/B yard. Vessel's first cargo was 98k MT gasoil, loaded in Ulsan with destination Lome where she performed a lot of back to back STS operations. The workload of all these operations became quickly heavy for the crew, thus after having performed 8 STS operations in a period of 10 days, it was agreed that Charterers would pay for one extra deck officer, two deck ratings and one engine rating  to remain in compliance with MLC and rest hours regulations (300 USD / day for the extra crew and 5000 USD to get them onboard billed and paid by charterers).  After the initial voyage, Mercuria sub-chartered the vessel to Trafigura who in their turn engaged the vessel in Nigerian cabotage for Dangote Refinery.  Vessel's final voyage with Trafigura was Jubilee to Madre De Deus, where Captain signed a wrongly dated B/L which created some operational issues, however thanks to good cooperation from all parties involved SSSP, Mercuria, Trafigura, P&I club and Owners we found an acceptable solution to this problem. Upon re-delivery to Mercuria in Madre de Deus, Charterers ballasted the ship back to waf where she was once again engaged in Nigerian cabotage with Dangote. After 6 cargoes loaded at different Nigerian offshore terminals and all discharged at Dangote SPM, the vessel was redelivered to SSSP at Lagos Anchorage. 

 

The pre-calculation was based on the minimum TC period and the variance in TCE is explained by the increased number of days and the hire rate increase from $49k PD to $52.5k PD after the first 120 days. Moreover, The TC period was extended and the hire was further increased to $55.5k PD from day 181 and onward (as per addendum). Considering all the short voyages in waf and that most of them were partially loaded, it is difficult to draw any bigger conclusions with regards to the speed and consumption of the vessel. Per orbit voyage report, vessel had an average speed of 13.1 kts and and average consumption of 32.6 mt/day (incl. aux) in ballast condition. In laden condition, she consumed in avg. 35 mt/day (incl. aux) at an avg. speed of 12.7 kts. Impressive numbers for a suezmax! Furthermore, as vessel was idle for almost a month in Lome (due to all STS ops) and Mercuria did not allow Owners to perform a hull inspection/cleaning despite several attempts, the speed and consumption warranties thereafter shall not apply as per CP. With the mentioned information in mind, the likeliness of a performance claim is very low. 

 

Working with trading companies such as Mercuria and Trafigura can sometimes become quite challenging and requires full focus at all times, especially when both companies are involved at the same time. Having said that, except delays in getting some LOIs invoked and a few questionable re-delivery notices, which were at a later stage withdrawn, Mercuria and their charterers have been pretty cooperative. All in all, a nice TC resulting in great returns for the pool!
Q88 performed some support on this voyage/TCO. Just to fix the number of remaining days and the new TC rate. (03 Jan 24)
